---
title: "Predicting Outpatient Consultation Duration"
subtitle: "Capstone Project Summary — Hangu Outpatient Oncology Clinic"
author: "Ariana Butina"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 6.5
    fig-height: 4
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import json
from pathlib import Path

from IPython.display import Image

ARTIFACTS = Path("../ml/artifacts")


def load_json(name: str) -> dict:
    with open(ARTIFACTS / name) as f:
        return json.load(f)


clf_metrics = load_json("test_metrics_classification.json")
reg_metrics = load_json("test_metrics_regression.json")
metadata = load_json("metadata.json")
```

## Motivation

Time-Driven Activity-Based Costing (TDABC) needs one core input per activity:
**how long it takes.** This project builds two machine learning models on 6,637
historical outpatient oncology consultations to predict exactly that — supporting a
master's thesis applying TDABC to outpatient scheduling.

- **Classifier** — will this consultation be *long* or *short*, relative to the clinic's
  own history?
- **Regressor** — how many minutes will this consultation take?

## System Architecture

```
GitHub  ──▶  Render (FastAPI + scikit-learn)  ◀── fetch() ──  Vercel (React + Recharts)
  │
  └──▶  ml/ pipeline: load → clean → engineer → split → tune → evaluate → persist
```

- **ML pipeline**: pandas, scikit-learn, XGBoost (compared, not selected) — reproducible,
  leakage-audited, versioned artifacts
- **Backend**: FastAPI, serving both models behind separate, documented REST endpoints
- **Frontend**: React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind + Recharts, with a live prediction
  form and an interactive results dashboard
- **Deployment**: GitHub → Render (backend) and GitHub → Vercel (frontend), both
  auto-deploying on push

## Key Results

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
```{python}
#| label: fig-confusion-matrix
#| fig-cap: "Classification: confusion matrix (held-out test set)"
Image("figures/confusion_matrix.png")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-residuals
#| fig-cap: "Regression: residuals vs. predicted (held-out test set)"
Image("figures/residuals.png")
```
:::

| Task | Metric | Value |
|---|---|---|
| Classification | Accuracy | `{python} f"{clf_metrics['accuracy']:.3f}"` |
| Classification | ROC-AUC | `{python} f"{clf_metrics['roc_auc']:.3f}"` |
| Regression | MAE | `{python} f"{reg_metrics['mae']:.2f} min"` |
| Regression | R² | `{python} f"{reg_metrics['r2']:.3f}"` |

**Headline finding:** across both tasks, **patient visit history** (`Visit.No`,
`IsRepeatVisit`) is by far the strongest predictor — well ahead of diagnosis flags or
calendar/scheduling context. For TDABC, this points toward visit-history-based patient
segmentation as the more promising basis for outpatient cost-driver estimation, rather
than segmentation by day, month, or diagnosis.

**Honest limitation:** the regression model explains only a small share of duration
variance (R² ≈ 0.07); the available scheduling fields cannot capture the clinical
complexity that likely drives most of a consultation's actual length. Predictions should
inform scheduling and capacity planning, not clinical decisions.

## Live Demo

- **Frontend:** [frontend-psi-seven-76.vercel.app](https://frontend-psi-seven-76.vercel.app)
- **Backend API docs:** [hangu-consult-backend.onrender.com/docs](https://hangu-consult-backend.onrender.com/docs)
- **Source code:** [github.com/arianabutina-source/hangu-consult-time-ml](https://github.com/arianabutina-source/hangu-consult-time-ml)

## Takeaway

A fully reproducible, leakage-audited ML pipeline — trained, evaluated once and honestly,
served through a documented API, and presented through an interactive dashboard — turns a
single clinic's scheduling data into an actionable signal for TDABC-style cost-driver
planning, while being transparent about exactly how far that signal goes.
